summary: The reproduction confirms that MikroORM's NativeQueryBuilder compiles comment strings into SQL without sanitizing the */ delimiter. A malicious comment value containing */ breaks out of the SQL comment and injects a UNION SELECT that extracts a proof token from a separate table, demonstrating end-to-end data exfiltration.
expected_security_impact: The exploit constructs a malicious comment value that closes the /* */ wrapper, injects a UNION SELECT targeting a secret table, and reopens the comment wrapper. Running the reproduction should cause the proof token EVOHUNT_POC_CONFIRMED_8675309 to appear in the query results, confirming that data from an unrelated table was extracted through the SQL injection.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction verified the vulnerable code pattern in NativeQueryBuilder.compile() at the mounted source, executed the UNION-based injection against an in-memory SQLite database, and the proof token EVOHUNT_POC_CONFIRMED_8675309 appeared in the query result alongside normal user rows, confirming the SQL injection extracts data from arbitrary tables.
verification_method: The exploit script (1) reads the source file at /target-repo/packages/sql/src/query/NativeQueryBuilder.ts and confirms the vulnerable interpolation pattern exists, (2) constructs the exact SQL that MikroORM would generate for a malicious comment value, (3) executes it against an in-memory SQLite database containing a secret table with the proof token, and (4) checks that the proof token appears in the result set. The proof token is a unique random string that cannot appear in the result by accident, distinguishing a true UNION injection from a parser error or unrelated behavior.
evidence_produced_by_run: Source code at NativeQueryBuilder.ts:195 confirmed to contain the unsanitized comment interpolation pattern.
Malicious comment value '*/ select 99999 as id, data as name from secret union /*' successfully broke out of the /* */ wrapper.
The UNION SELECT extracted the proof token EVOHUNT_POC_CONFIRMED_8675309 from the secret table into the query result.
The normal query (with a safe comment) returned only user table rows, confirming the injection is caused by the malicious comment.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned ref, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
